situation: calibration.js uses new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10) for 'today' (line 104) and new Date(Date.now()-86400000).toISOString().slice(0,10) for 'yesterday' (line 114). toISOString() is always UTC, so during CST 00:00-07:59 the date shifts -1 day. This causes calibrateWeights() to save weight records under wrong date keys, and runDailyCalibration() to fetch weather for the wrong day. The project already has localDateCST() in date-utils.js that correctly uses Intl.DateTimeFormat with Asia/Shanghai timezone — routes.js uses it everywhere, but calibration.js did not import it.
action: Import localDateCST from date-utils.js; replace line 104 `new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10)` with `localDateCST(0)`; replace line 114 `new Date(Date.now()-86400000).toISOString().slice(0,10)` with `localDateCST(-1)`. Single file change, fixes both H-001 and H-002.
outcome_evidence: opt-worktree.sh committed to opt-calibration-1783395468, pushed to origin/auto/opt-calibration-1783395468. Diff: +1 import line, 2 lines changed (toISOString→localDateCST). No other files touched.
next_suggestion: Continue dispatching pending route-fixes from audit-2026-07-08-046. Next priority: SP-ENG-H-003 (prediction-engine.js setWeights NaN guard) or SP-SCR-H-001 (post_xhs_browseruse.py cookie plaintext umask).

audit_findings.finding: calibrateWeights uses new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10) which is UTC — shifts -1 day during CST 00:00-07:59, saving weight records under wrong date key
runDailyCalibration yesterday calculation uses new Date(Date.now()-86400000).toISOString().slice(0,10) — same UTC shift bug, fetches weather for wrong day
